phase3_findings.description: lib/test/run.sh:316 reworded comment said the suite has 'python3 word counts' but the surviving python3 counts are non-whitespace CHARACTER counts (_nonws_count); the word counters (_rb_words/_raf_words) were deleted by this change.

Four surviving pointers to the renamed 'Prose cutover' section (implement.md section was renamed to 'Keeping prompt prose lean (advisory)'): CLAUDE.md:83, .devflow/prompt-extensions/review-and-fix.md:19 (broken markdown anchor), docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:476 (also a false 'complete sole-owner bar lives there' claim), and CONTRIBUTING.md:373 (missed by the reviewers, caught by orchestrator full-tree grep).

fix_decisions.note: repointed all 4 'Prose cutover' references to 'Keeping prompt prose lean'; sole-owner discipline survives in CLAUDE.md Helper-cutover bullet; dropped false 'complete sole-owner bar' claim

phase3_findings.description: Two pre-existing (diff-untouched) comments still cite the deleted 'review-bundle word ceiling' as a live constraint: lib/test/cloud_writer_contract.py:1020 and lib/test/extract-command-heads.py:518. The retirement sweep missed them.

shadow.reason: shadow surfaced 1 corroborated non-Critical comment_drift finding (2 stale word-ceiling clauses); fixed in commit 03763498; the final-pass shadow reviewer independently reviewed the POST-fix tree and verified the clauses are gone, giving the fix delta its independent pass. No Critical, no unresolved Important.
